title: Add toggle-all expand/collapse for bead list tree
description: Add a keybinding to expand or collapse all tree nodes in the bead list panel at once. If any nodes are currently collapsed, expand all. If all are expanded, collapse all. This is a convenience shortcut for navigating large bead trees. Add a keybinding (e.g. 'a' for 'all' or 'E' for expand/collapse all) that toggles between fully expanded and fully collapsed states. Should work with the Tree widget introduced in B0130.
acceptance_criteria: A keybinding toggles all tree nodes between fully expanded and fully collapsed
If any nodes are collapsed, the toggle expands all
If all nodes are expanded, the toggle collapses all
The keybinding is documented in the help overlay

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented action_toggle_all_tree_nodes bound to E key. Logic: checks if any expandable node (has_children) is in _collapsed_bead_ids; if so, clears the set (expand all); otherwise, adds all expandable IDs (collapse all). Then repopulates the tree. Added E to help overlay text.
handoff_summary.remaining: Test and documentation followup beads should cover unit tests for the new toggle-all behavior and help text update.
handoff_summary.risks: None significant. The feature is additive and uses the existing _collapsed_bead_ids mechanism.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should add unit tests for the toggle-all behavior; documentation agent should update any user-facing docs.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: tui.py is shared with other TUI beads — merges may conflict around BINDINGS list and help overlay text.

conflict_risks: tui.py is shared with other TUI beads — merges may conflict around BINDINGS list and help overlay text.

execution_history.summary: Added `E` keybinding to toggle all bead tree nodes between fully expanded and fully collapsed. If any expandable nodes are collapsed, pressing `E` expands all; if all are expanded, it collapses all. The keybinding is documented in the help overlay. All 126 existing tests pass.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run only the specific test files directly related to your changes, not the full test suite. Use `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v` to target individual test files rather than `discover`. Leave comprehensive test validation to the tester agent.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
